functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase mediating growth factor signaling
Drives tumor cell proliferation in lung adenocarcinoma
Oncogenic driver via activating mutations (e.g., G719A) in NSCLC
Implicated in neuronal survival and metabolic adaptation signaling
pubmed.druggability_notes: EGFR is a well-established therapeutic target. EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitors (afatinib) used for EGFR-mutant NSCLC; amivantamab (bispecific antibody) plus chemotherapy effective in uncommon EGFR G719A mutation. Molecular docking against EGFR PDB ID 1XKK shows phytochemical binding affinities (e.g., aspidinol -8.6 kcal/mol), indicating a druggable binding pocket for small-molecule inhibitors.

functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase binding EGF-family ligands (EGF, TGFA, AREG, EPGN, BTC, EREG, HBEGF)
Ligand binding triggers receptor homo- and heterodimerization and autophosphorylation on cytoplasmic residues
Recruits adapter protein GRB2 to activate downstream signaling cascades
Phosphorylates RGS16 to activate its GTPase activity, coupling EGFR to GPCR signaling
Phosphorylates MUC1, increasing its interaction with SRC and CTNNB1/beta-catenin
Positively regulates cell migration via interaction with CCDC88A/GIV retaining EGFR at the cell membrane
Functions as host cell receptor for virus entry
Role in learning, memory, and mammalian pain signaling
pathways: Epidermal growth factor receptor signaling pathway
uniprot.druggability_notes: EGFR is an ATP-binding tyrosine-protein kinase (nucleotide-binding, ATP binding) with a defined kinase domain, making it a well-established druggable target. The text notes disease variants and proto-oncogene status; it is a classic therapeutic target for tyrosine kinase inhibitors, though specific drug classes are not named in the provided text.

functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase binding EGF-family ligands (EGF, TGFA, AREG, EPGN, BTC, EREG, HBEGF)
Ligand binding triggers receptor homo- and heterodimerization and autophosphorylation of cytoplasmic residues
Recruits adapter proteins such as GRB2 to activate downstream signaling cascades
Activates RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK, PI3K-AKT, PLCgamma-PKC and STAT signaling modules
May activate NF-kappa-B signaling cascade
Phosphorylates RGS16 to activate its GTPase activity, coupling EGFR to GPCR signaling
Phosphorylates MUC1 and increases its interaction with SRC and CTNNB1/beta-catenin
Positively regulates cell migration via interaction with CCDC88A/GIV; acts as receptor for HCV entry in hepatocytes
pathways: Constitutive Signaling by Ligand-Responsive EGFR Cancer Variants (R-HSA-1236382)
Inhibition of Signaling by Overexpressed EGFR (R-HSA-5638303)

disease_associations.disease: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

opentargets.druggability_notes: EGFR is a receptor tyrosine kinase and well-established therapeutic target. Text describes a cytoplasmic kinase domain with key autophosphorylation residues and ligand-binding ectodomain. Oncogenic variants include EGFRvIII and ligand-responsive cancer variants (R-HSA-1236382, R-HSA-5637810), which are clinically targeted. Pathways for inhibition of overexpressed EGFR are noted (R-HSA-5638303), consistent with tyrosine kinase inhibitor and anti-receptor antibody drug classes.
